examples.label: Superflex, IDP, Deep roster, Uploaded rankings

scoringEmphasis.reason: Superflex or 2QB structure increases quarterback demand.
IDP slots require defensive position planning instead of a single generic pool.
Heavy flex or deep bench settings increase running back and wide receiver depth value.

rosterConstructionPlan.guidance: Superflex and 2QB formats make quarterback structure a core planning lane.
Blackbird leans toward capturing format-sensitive value before forcing low-priority slots.
Use tier risk and market value pockets to build starters and flexible depth.
Fill special positions late while using remaining bench slots for upside and coverage.
Build defensive coverage by position group, then react to tier risk.

draftSlotStrategy.slotStrategySummary: Early-slot strategy preview: prioritize an anchor start and account for a 22-pick wait back.

roundPickWindows.label: early anchor window
roundPickWindows.guidance: Blackbird leans toward an anchor start while planning for the long Round 1-to-Round 2 wait.

roundPickWindows.label: middle-round value windows
roundPickWindows.guidance: Timing signal: compare value pockets against a max snake wait near 22 picks.

roundPickWindows.label: IDP timing window
roundPickWindows.guidance: Use IDP tier risk and named targets before waiting through another full turn.

roundPickWindows.label: K/DST timing window
roundPickWindows.guidance: Keep K/DST in late fill windows unless scoring settings create a measurable format signal.

roundWindowPlanBySlot.guidance: Blackbird leans toward an anchor start while planning for the long Round 1-to-Round 2 wait.

timingSignals: Turn pick: Blackbird leans toward paired-position planning.

roundWindowPlan.guidance: Blackbird leans toward an anchor start while planning for the long Round 1-to-Round 2 wait.
Monitor QB tier risk before long snake waits.
Timing signal: compare value pockets against a max snake wait near 22 picks.

Plan IDP starts around tier risk while accounting for lower projection confidence.

contingencyPlans.trigger: If a QB tier dries up before the next turn
contingencyPlans.response: pivot to the strongest non-QB value pocket and revisit QB on the next timing signal.

contingencyPlans.trigger: If an IDP tier becomes thin
contingencyPlans.response: avoid waiting without named targets and compare DL/LB/DB coverage separately.

contingencyPlans.trigger: If RB/WR value falls below market
contingencyPlans.response: let value pocket evidence compete with roster need before filling a lower-value slot.

contingencyPlans.trigger: If wait guidance lacks named targets
contingencyPlans.response: treat the position as monitor-only and prefer stronger tier or value signals.

specialPositionGuidance.guidance: Plan IDP by position group; low-confidence baselines make tier risk and named targets more important.

riskNotes: H11.0 is design/foundation only and read-only.
True historical completed-draft outcome validation remains unavailable.
explanationFragments: Read-only strategy preview built from league format, roster settings, H10 timing signals, tiers, and market context.
Blackbird leans are deterministic and do not mutate draft state, projections, or player ordering.
Timing signal and tier risk are pre-draft planning aids; live H10 recommendations remain separate.

notes: Exact pre-draft strategy improves when raw draft slot, team count, round count, and roster slots are loaded.
H10 validation artifacts provide compact timing and recommendation rows but not every raw league scoring key.
